ety: for A. سنبلة sumbulat, n. of un. fr. sumbul, 'ear of corn'
def: s.m. The sign Virgo, Spica virginis.
s.m. corr. of sammuʼl-fār, q.v.s.v. samm.
adj. Very corpulent.
adj. Suitable, harmonizing; of pleasing manners, affable, sociable.

s.m. The sacred mountain Meru (allegorically represented as composed of gold and gems, and the residence of the gods); — the north pole (in astron. works); — a leader; the large or centre bead in a rosary (syn. inām)
subs: and sumeru-gir, s.m. The mountain Sumeru or Meru.

def: s.m. The sacred mountain Meru (allegorically represented as composed of gold and gems, and the residence of the gods); — the north pole (in astron. works); — a leader; the large or centre bead in a rosary (syn. inām)
subs: and sumeru-gir, s.m. The mountain Sumeru or Meru.

def: s.m. The black bee, the humble-bee.

ety: Prk. सुसरो; S. स्मरः, rt. स्मृ
def: s.m. Remembrance, recollection (cf. sumaran and sumarnā).
part. Remembered, recollected, called to mind; mentioned, made mention of.
s.m. A good friend, a friend; — a proper name.

ety: pl. of aṣammu, 'deaf'; rt. صمّ 'to be deaf'
def: adj. Deaf, having a stoppage of the ear
subs: (corr. gum-ṣum), adj. Deaf and dumb; speechless.

def: s.m. Sumach, Rhus coriaria, or its berry; — porphyry, the hardest species of marble.
wety: From Arabic سُمَّاق (summāq).

def: s.m. Hymn; a sacrifice; joy, happiness.
s.m. A chanter of hymns.

ety: sūm, q.v.+Prk. प्पणं or प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं), or त्व+कं
def: s.m. Miserliness, niggardliness, stinginess, avarice; meanness.

ety: sūm, q.v.+Prk. प्पणं or प्पणअं = S. त्वं (Vedic त्वनं), or त्व+कं
def: s.m. Miserliness, niggardliness, stinginess, avarice; meanness.

ety: sūm, q.v.+ṛā = Prk. डओ = S. र+कः
def: adj. (f. -ī) & s.m. Ugly; avaricious, miserly, niggardly, stingy, penurious; — a miser, niggard ( = sūm).
adj. & s.m. contrac. of sūmṛā, q.v.
s.f. The hissing of moist wood when burning; simmering ( = sunsun, sansan, and sansanāhaṭ).
wety: Borrowed from Arabic سِمْسِم (simsim), borrowed from Aramaic שׁוּמְשֻׁומָא (šumššumā), borrowed from Akkadian 𒊭𒈦𒌑𒈬 (šamaššammum), from šaman + šammum.

def: s.f. = sūm-panā, q.v.

s.m. A good or beautiful mouth; a handsome face; — the mark or scratch of a finger-nail; — adj. & s.m. (f. -ā or -ī), Handsome-faced, lovely, pleasing, agreeable, propitious; having a beautiful mouth; — a handsome man; a learned man or teacher.
s.f. A handsome woman; — a mirror.
s.f. A handsome woman ( = su-mukhā); a species of the tri-shṭubh metre.
adj. (f. -ā), Having a handsome face; well adorned.
s.m. pl. (of samn, q.v.), Butters, ghīs, &c.
s.m. An eighth, an eighth part or portion.
s.m. Narration (prob. corr. of A. sumūr, inf. n. of سمر; see samar); — a small lake (prob. corr. of P. shamar).

adj. & s.m. Void, empty, blank; deserted, desolate, lonely, dreary; still, quiet; silent, speechless; without sensation, insensible, numb, paralyzed; — a void, a blank; a cypher, nought; a dot, point, mark; stillness, silence; loneliness, dreariness; a state of insensibility, fainting-fit, swoon, coma
subs: adj. Still, lonely, deserted, dreary, desolate, &c.
v.t. To render insensible or torpid, to benumb; to paralyze
adj. & s.m. (f. -ī), Rendering insensible; (in Med.) narcotic; — a narcotic
s.m. A dangerous fainting-fit, a death-like swoon to which lying-in women are subject, and from which they seldom recover
v.n. To become numb or devoid of sensation; to be paralyzed (with fear, &c.).
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian سن (sinn), from Arabic سِنّ (sinn).

def: adj. & s.m. Void of; empty, &c. ( = sun, q.v.)
subs: or sūn sādhnā, or sūn kheṅćnā, v.n. To preserve or keep silence, to be silent.

def: s.m. The uvula or soft palate; — a slaughter-house, any place where animals are killed or are liable to be destroyed.
adj. & s.m. corr. of śūnya, q.v.; and see sun, sūnā.
adj. & s.m. Void, empty, &c. ( = sūn, or sun, q.v.); hollow; deserted, desolate; ruined; unoccupied, untenanted; plain, unadorned; — a void, blank, &c. (see sun)
subs: v.t. To make void, to empty, &c.
(-ko), To leave void or desolate, &c.; to devastate, lay waste, ruin.
ex: میں رات کو دس بجے سوتا ہوں۔
ma͠i rāt ko das baje sotā hū̃
I sleep at ten o'clock at night.
میرا بھائی پڑھتے پڑھتے سو گیا۔
My brother fell asleep while reading.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀼𑀡𑁆𑀡 (suṇṇa), from Sanskrit शून्य (śūnya, “empty”). Compare Punjabi سنجا / ਸੁੰਞਾ (suñā), Nepali सुन् (sun), Gujarati સૂનું (sūnũ), Marathi सुना (sunā). Related to سُنْسان (sunsān).

def: s.m. (f. -ī), A dog
ex: میں رات کو دس بجے سوتا ہوں۔
ma͠i rāt ko das baje sotā hū̃
I sleep at ten o'clock at night.
میرا بھائی پڑھتے پڑھتے سو گیا۔
My brother fell asleep while reading.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀲𑀼𑀡𑁆𑀡 (suṇṇa), from Sanskrit शून्य (śūnya, “empty”). Compare Punjabi سنجا / ਸੁੰਞਾ (suñā), Nepali सुन् (sun), Gujarati સૂનું (sūnũ), Marathi सुना (sunā). Related to سُنْسان (sunsān).

def: adj. See śūnya, and sūnā.
part. (f. -ī), Heard, &c.
subs: v.t. To treat as unheard that which was heard, to pretend not to hear, to turn a deaf ear to, to disregard
part. adj. & s.m. (f. sunī-sunāʼī), What one has heard or been told of; rumoured, reported; — hear-say, rumour.
wety: From Sanskrit [Term?].

def: s.m. Name of a mountain, part of the ranges of Southern India.
adj. (f. -ī) = sunahrā, q.v.

ety: Prk. सुवस्णइल्लओ (with h inserted); S. सुवर्ण+इल+कः
def: adj. Of gold, golden; gilded; gold-coloured
subs: (or sunahrī) pānī, s.m. Gilding; wash or coating of gold
s.m. Golden colour or hue; orange colour.

ety: Prk. सुवस्णइल्लओ (with h inserted); S. सुवर्ण+इल+कः
def: adj. Of gold, golden; gilded; gold-coloured
subs: (or sunahrī) pānī, s.m. Gilding; wash or coating of gold
s.m. Golden colour or hue; orange colour.

def: s.f. Hearing; a hearing; justice
subs: v.n. To come within hearing, to be heard.

def: adj. (f. -ī), Golden, &c. ( = sunahrā, q.v.).
s.m. Golden-huedness, &c.
s.m. A young dog; a dog.
s.m. corr. of su-nakshatra, q.v.
s.m. A fortunate star or constellation.
s.f. Name of the second night of the civil month.
s.m. Good name, good repute, reputation.
adj. Well-named; of good repute.
adj. Well-named; of good repute.
s.f. (dialec.) Hearing, listening
v.t. To cause to hear or to be heard, to cause to be made public, to proclaim, announce; to inform, advise, tell, bid; to say or repeat (a lesson); — to speak at; to warn; to rate, rebuke, chide
adj. (f. -ā), Pleasing well, affording pleasure, delighting; — s.m. Name of Baldev's club.
s.f. A woman; an epithet of Umā.
v.t. = sunānā, q.v.

ety: prob. for सुनावट = S. शून्य + āwaṭ = Prk. अवट्टी = S. वृत्तिः
def: s.f. Stillness, loneliness, dreariness, desolateness.
s.m. A worker in gold, a goldsmith (also written sonār)
subs: s.m. The goldsmith's quarter (of a town).
wety: Inherited from Middle Hindi سنار (sunār) (c. 1609) / سونار (svnar /⁠sūnār⁠/) (c. 1572) , from Apabhramsa 𑆱𑆶𑆤𑇀𑆤𑆳𑆫 (sunnāra), from Prakrit 𑀲𑀼𑀡𑁆𑀡𑀸𑀭 (suṇṇāra) from Sanskrit सुवर्णकार (suvarṇakāra), compound of सुवर्ण (suvárṇa) + कार (kāra).

def: s.m. (rustic) = sunār, q.v.
s.f. A goldsmith's wife; a woman of the goldsmith caste.
s.f. The business of a goldsmith; the pay or hire of a goldsmith; — a goldsmith's wife ( = sunārnī).
s.f. A good woman; a handsome or beautiful woman; a beauty.
s.f. = su-nāṛī, q.v.

ety: prob. S. सु+नार+इकः; but cf. anārī, to which it is opp.
def: adj. & s.m. Clever, skilful, expert, adept; alert, handy; neat; — a clever man, an expert, an adept.

ety: prob. S. सु+नार+इकः; but cf. anārī, to which it is opp.
def: adj. & s.m. Clever, skilful, expert, adept; alert, handy; neat; — a clever man, an expert, an adept.
s.f. A clever woman.
s.f. A goldsmith's wife; a woman of the goldsmith caste.
s.m. An epithet of Indra.
s.m. An epithet of Indra.
s.m. A goldsmith's son.

ety: sun(nā) + āwat = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. इतव्य+क+त्वं
def: s.f. Hearing ( = sunāʼī).
v.t. = sunānā, q.v.
s.f. Intelligence of the death of someone in a foreign country.
s.m. Good conduct; good policy.
adj. & s.m. (f. -ā), Having beautiful eyes, beautiful-eyed; — a deer.
s.f. A woman; an epithet of the wife of king Janak.
s.f. Insensibility of the skin, Tactus imminutus; elephantiasis (a constitutional disease, manifesting itself locally, and usually on the legs and scrotum).
s.f. = sunbahrī, q.v.
s.m. Trunk (of an elephant; see sūṅṛ).
s.m. The exudation from an elephant's trunk; an elephant's trunk ( = sūṅr).
s.f. An elephant's trunk
subs: s.m. An elephant (a term used by women).

def: s.m. 1˚ A small brown insect which destroys grain (esp. gram), a weevil; — 2˚ = sūṅḍkā or sūṅṛkā, q.v.
s.f. An elephant's trunk; — spirituous liquor; a tavern; a harlot; the stalk of the lotus.
adj. (f. -ī), Handsome, beautiful, lovely, charming, elegant, comely, seemly, fine, neat; agreeable, pleasing, delicious; excellent, good, virtuous, pure
subs: s.m. A graceful gait; — adj. Moving gracefully, or with a pleasing gait
s.m. An epithet of Krishn ( = śyām-sundar)
s.m. The title of the fifth section of the Rāmāyan
wety: Borrowed from Sanskrit सुन्दर (sundara). First attested in c. 1611 as Middle Hindi سندر (sndr /⁠sundar⁠/).

ety: āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका; — in ˚tāʼī, ī = S. इका
def: Beauty handsomeness, elegance, comeliness, gracefulness; excellence, goodness, virtuousness.

ety: āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका; — in ˚tāʼī, ī = S. इका
def: Beauty handsomeness, elegance, comeliness, gracefulness; excellence, goodness, virtuousness.

ety: āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका; — in ˚tāʼī, ī = S. इका
def: Beauty handsomeness, elegance, comeliness, gracefulness; excellence, goodness, virtuousness.
s.f. A beautiful woman, a beauty; — the tree Heritiera minor (a small timber-tree); turmeric; a species of metre; — beauty, &c. ( = sundarāʼī, q.v.).

ety: āʼī = Prk. अइआ = S. ता+इका; — in ˚tāʼī, ī = S. इका
def: Beauty handsomeness, elegance, comeliness, gracefulness; excellence, goodness, virtuousness.
s.f. (dialec.) = sūṅḍ, q.v.

ety: S. सुगन्ध+āwaṭ = Prk. अवट्टं = S. क+वृत्तं
def: s.m. Smell, perfume, odour, scent; an earthy smell (syn. soṅdhāhaṭ).
s.f. A small insect destructive to grain, weevil ( = sūṅḍā); the navel (syn. tūṅḍī); the uvula; swelling or enlargement of any gland; — spirituous liquor; the manufacture of spirituous liquor.
s.m. A distiller and vendor of spirituous liquors; — a dealer in rice, a grocer
subs: s.f. A tax on the vendors of spirituous liquors.

def: s.m. A pad placed under a saddle, saddle-pad, saddle-cloth; a pack-saddle, dorser; hump (of a camel, or buffalo, &c.).
s.m. & f. A box, trunk, chest, coffer, case; a coffin.
wety: Borrowed from Classical Persian صَنْدُوق (sandūq), borrowed from Arabic صَنْدُوق (ṣandūq), borrowed from Aramaic צנדוק (ṣəndūq, “coffer, especially one storing documents, safe-guarding precious items”), borrowed from Ancient Greek σῠνθήκη (sŭnthḗkē), from σῠντῐ́θημῐ (sŭntĭ́thēmĭ, “to place or put together”), from συν- (sun-) + τίθημι (títhēmi).

def: s.m. Thin, or fine, silk brocade; a rich silk, satin.
adj. Made of brocade, brocaded.

ety: prob. another form of sūṅḍkā, q.v.
def: s.m. The clitoris; a protuberance, a hump.
s.f. Smelling, inhaling; smell, odour, scent.

ety: sūṅgh(nā) + ā = iʼā = iwā = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+क+इकः
def: adj. (f. -ī), Stanch (as a dog); — s.m. A stanch hound; — a person who is (supposed to be) able to discover hidden treasure or grain, or good water, by the smell of the earth; a detective; a setter of jewels.

ety: sunghā(nā) + āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं; — and āʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. Scent, odour, perfume, fragrance.
s.f. Smelling; — anything to smell; snuff, &c. ( = sūṅghnī).
v.t. corr. of suṅghānā, q.v.
v.t. To cause to smell, to make (one) smell, to get (one) to smell.

ety: sunghā(nā) + āwaṭ = Prk. एअव्वअट्टं = S. (इ)तव्य+क+त्वं; — and āʼī = Prk. एअव्वअइओ = S. (इ)तव्य+ता+इका
def: s.f. Scent, odour, perfume, fragrance.
v.t. = sūṅghnā, q.v.

ety: sūṅgh˚ = Prk. समग्घ(इ) or समग्घे(इ) = S. समाघ्रा(ति), rt. सम्+आ+घ्रा
def: v.t. To smell, sniff, snuff, inhale, scent, nose
subs: (sūṅghtā, imperf. part. of sūṅghnā), v.n. To go sniffing about; to prowl about.

def: s.f. = sūṅghnī, q.v.
s.f. Anything to be smelt or sniffed; scent; snuff; smelling-salts; snuff-box
subs: (-kī), To sniff; to snuff; to get scent (of), to nuzzle (see sungun).

def: v.t. (dialec. or colloq.) = sūṅghnā, q.v.
v.t. (dialec. & colloq.) = sūṅghnā, q.v.

ety: sūṅg = sūṅgā+rā = Prk. डओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. A buffalo-calf (lit. 'the little humped one').

ety: sūṅg = sūṅgā+rā = Prk. डओ = S. र+कः
def: s.m. A buffalo-calf (lit. 'the little humped one').
s.f. Scent, secret information, private intelligence; spying; inspection
subs: (-kī), To obtain secret information (of), to play the spy (on); to take an inspection (of).

def: s.f. A female dog, a bitch.
s.f. Sound sleep; healthy slumber.
s.m. lit. 'Very blue'; the blue shrike; the emerald; the sapphire.
adj. (f. -ā), Well-conducted, well-behaved; politic; prudent.
s.m. = next, q.v.
s.f. Good conduct or behaviour, good manners, propriety; good policy; prudence; — name of the wife of Uttanāpād and mother of Dhruva.
s.m. A fruiterer.
s.m. A kind of snake.
See سننا sunnā.
wety: From Sanskrit [Term?].

ety: sun˚ = Prk. सुण(इ) = S. श्टणो (ति), rt. श्रु
def: v.t. To get to hear, to come to know; to overhear; — sun-lenā, v.t. To hear, &c. ( = sunnā); to get to hear, to overhear
subs: s.m. Hearer, listener; (in gram.) the second person.
wety: Urdu verb set سُنْنا (sunnā) سُنانا (sunānā) سُنْوانا (sunvānā) Inherited from Sanskrit शृणोति (śṛṇoti), from Proto-Indo-Iranian *ćr̥náwti, from Proto-Indo-European *ḱl̥néwti, from *ḱlew- + *-né-.

def: s.m. pl. (of ṣāněʻ, q.v.), Handicraftsmen, manufacturers, artificers, artisans, mechanics, workmen.
s.m. A blank; a vacuum; a cypher, nought ( = sun); a mute (in gram.).
wety: From Sanskrit [Term?].

def: s.m. (dialec.) = sonā, 'gold,' q.v.
wety: From Sanskrit [Term?].

ety: for A. سنة, v.n. fr. سن 'to form or fashion,' 'to institute,' &c.
def: s.f. Mode or manner of acting, course or rule of conduct or of life, right way (of life); statute, ordinance (of religion), rite, an obligatory religious ceremony; circumcision; a practice or saying (or the practices and sayings collectively) of Mohammad as handed down by tradition, the traditions of Mohammad; — the prayers enjoined by Mohammad
wety: Borrowed from Arabic سُنَّة (sunna).

def: adj. & s.m. Lawful; — an orthodox Moḥammadan, one who receives the sunnat or traditionary portion of Moḥammadan law; one who reveres equally the four successors of Moḥammad (hence, also called ćahār-yārī. The Turks are Sunnīs; the Persians are Shīʻas, q.v.).
adj. & s.m. (Braj) = sūnā, q.v.
adj. & s.m. (Braj) = sūnā, q.v.
s.f. = sūṅḍ, q.v.
s.m. = sūṅdā, and sūṅḍkā, qq.v.
s.f. & s.m. = sūṅḍī, q v.
s.m. = sūṅdā, and sūṅḍkā, qq.v.
s.m. A porpoise.

ety: S. शून्य+सन्नः; or redupl. of शून्य
def: adj. See s.v. sun.
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit *𑀲𑀼𑀡𑁆𑀡𑀲𑀸𑀡 (*suṇṇasāṇa), from Sanskrit शून्यस्थान n (śūnyasthāna, “empty place”), from शून्य (śūnya, “empty”) + स्थान (sthāna, “place”). Also see سُونا (sūnā) and تھانَہ (thāna).

def: adj. = sun-sān, q.v.s.v. sun.
s.m. A kind of ornament.
wety: From Sanskrit संसार (saṃsāra).

def: s.m. = sunsār, q.v.
s.f. Sniffing, snuffing
s.m. Snuffling (from cold), stuffiness of the nose, a cold, catarrh.
s.f. A mole (the animal).

ety: for sūṅṭh; prob. akin to S. शुण्ठ् 'to be or become dry'; cf. soṅth, 'dry ginger'
def: s.f. Silence; — adj. Silent
subs: or sūṅṭ mārnā, v.n. To keep silence, to be silent
v.n. To depart in silence, go away quietly.

def: s.f. contrac. of next, q.v.
s.f. = śuṇṭhi; and soṅṭh, qq.v.
s.f. Dry ginger ( = soṅṭh).

v.t. To rub down; to rub (the body), to shampoo; to strip the leaves off a branch, or off vegetables, &c. (by running the hand down it or them); to strip, lay bare; to draw or unsheath (a sword); to straighten (a wire, &c., by rubbing the hand down it); to throw out, bale out (water); to drain, exhaust.

ety: S. शुण्ठि+वटकः; — or soṅṭh, q.v.+Prk. अउघ्घओ = S. क+उल+कः
def: s.m. Ginger-bread; a preparation of ginger, molasses, sugar, &c. (given to puerperal women, &c.).

v.t. To rub down; to rub (the body), to shampoo; to strip the leaves off a branch, or off vegetables, &c. (by running the hand down it or them); to strip, lay bare; to draw or unsheath (a sword); to straighten (a wire, &c., by rubbing the hand down it); to throw out, bale out (water); to drain, exhaust.
s.m. A son; a younger brother; — the sun.
s.f. (dialec.) A daughter-in-law.
s.f. A daughter.
(inf. n. of سنح 'to present a side,' &c.), s.m.f.(?) Happening, appearing, presenting itself, manifestation, an occurrence.
s.m. The red jujube-plant (syn. ber).
s.m. Hearer, listener ( = sunne-wālā).
adj. & s.m. = sūn, sun, and śūnya, qq.v.
s.f. (see śūnya), A hollow reed.
adj. & s.m. Empty, void, hollow; vacant, &c. (see sūnā, sūn, and sun); — a dot, a cypher, &c.
subs: s.m. 'An affirmer of non-existence,' an atheist; a Buddhist.

def: corr. of śūnya, q.v.
s.f. = S شونيتو शून्यत्व śūnyatva, s.m. Emptiness, voidness, vacuity, non-existence, unreality, vanity, the false or illusory nature of all existence.
s.m. = S شونيتا शून्यता śūnyatā, s.f. Emptiness, voidness, vacuity, non-existence, unreality, vanity, the false or illusory nature of all existence.
s.m. A kind of winnowing basket, a kind of wicker receptacle which, when shaken about, serves as a fan for winnowing; a wooden scoop (suspended in a frame) for throwing up water for irrigation
subs: s.f. lit. 'Having finger-nails like winnowing fans,' name of the sister of Rāvana (she fell in love with Rāma, and, being rejected by him, attacked Sītā, but was beaten off and mutilated by Lakshmaṇa; in revenge she incited her brother to carry off Sītā).

def: s.m. Soup, broth; split pease; sauce, condiment; a cook; a vessel, pot, pan; an arrow
subs: corr. sūp-sāsra, s.m. The art of cooking

def: s.m. A swallow, Hirundo apus batassia.
s.m. corr. of swa-pać = شوپچ śva-pać, q.v.

r: supn, supan, sapn, sapan, supnā, sapnā
ety: Prk. सुप्पणो and सुप्पणओ; S. स्वप्नः and स्वप्न+कः
def: s.m. Sleep; dreaming, a dream, vision

r: supnā, sapnā, supn, supan, sapn, sapan
ety: Prk. सुप्पणो and सुप्पणओ; S. स्वप्नः and स्वप्न+कः
def: s.m. Sleep; dreaming, a dream, vision
ex: تھوڑی دیر کے بعد، اُن کے سپنے سچّے نکلے۔
thoṛī der ke ba'ad, un ke sapne sacce nikle.
After a little while, their dreams became true.
ناخدا بننا ہمیشہ اس کا سپنا تھا۔
nāxudā bannā hameśa us kā sapnā thā.
It was always his dream to become a pilot.
wety: Cognates include Latin somnus, Ancient Greek ὕπνος (húpnos), Old English swefn, and (English sweven).

def: adj. corr. of śūrpa-nakhā, q v.s.v. شورپ śūrpa, and see also s.v. sūp.
adj. Distinctly visible, manifest, apparent; public.
part. adj. Well-pleased; very gracious or favourable.
s.f. Good reputation, fame, celebrity; establishment or erection (of a temple, or idol, &c.); installation, consecration; — a species of metre containing four lines of five syllables each.
part. Sleeping, asleep; paralyzed, numbed, insensible, senseless; — Sleep, deep or sound sleep (see supti)
subs: (˚ta+ut˚), part. Aroused or arisen from sleep, just arisen after sleep.

def: s.f. Sleeping, sleep, deep or sound sleep; sleepiness, drowsiness; numbness, insensibility, paralysis.
part. See s.v. supta.
adj. Pure, clean.

ety: v.n. fr. sipurdan; rt. Zend śpar = S. स्फर or स्फुर; cf. S. सम्प्रदा

def: s.m. The spleen, milt.

s.m. Sound, voice, tone, tune; harmony; music, melody, air, song; note (of the musical scale), the gamut; a bass, drone, or accompaniment to the shāhnāʼī; — air breathed through the nostrils, nasal sound or twang; snoring; — accent, tone; — a vowel

or maddham sur, s.m. The tenor; contralto

def: s.m. Tenesmus.

Doublet of سِرِن٘ج (siriñj). Cognates Compare with Odia ସୁରଙ୍ଗ (suraṅga), Bengali সুড়ঙ্গ (śuṛoṅgo), Assamese সুৰুঙা (xuruṅa) and Punjabi سُرَن٘گ (suraṉg) / ਸੁਰੰਗ (suraṅg).

Doublet of سِرِن٘ج (siriñj). Cognates Compare with Odia ସୁରଙ୍ଗ (suraṅga), Bengali সুড়ঙ্গ (śuṛoṅgo), Assamese সুৰুঙা (xuruṅa) and Punjabi سُرَن٘گ (suraṉg) / ਸੁਰੰਗ (suraṅg).

ety: for A. صورة, v.n. fr. صوّر 'to form,' &c., ii of صور 'to turn, or incline,' &c.
def: s.f. Form, fashion, figure, shape, semblance, guise; appearance, aspect; face, countenance; prospect, probability; sign, indication; external state (of a thing); state, condition (of a thing), case, predicament, circumstance; effigy, image, statue, picture, portrait; plan, sketch; mental image, idea; — species; specific character, essence; — means; mode, manner, way, (in Gram.) mood

conj. In case (of, or that); provided that.
